([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Regio Esercito*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Royal Army', **RE**) was the [land force](/wiki/Land_force "Land force") of the [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy"), established with the [proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Proclamation_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy "Proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy"). During the 19th century Italy started to unify into one country, and in 1861 [Manfredo Fanti](/wiki/Manfredo_Fanti "Manfredo Fanti") signed a decree creating the [Army of the Two Sicilies](/wiki/Army_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Army of the Two Sicilies"). This newly created army's first task was to defend its territorial gains against [Legitimists](/wiki/Legitimist "Legitimist") in southern Italy, who remained loyal to [Francis II of the Two Sicilies](/wiki/Francis_II_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Francis II of the Two Sicilies"). The Army of the Two Sicilies also waged what many modern historians now consider a [civil war](/wiki/Civil_war "Civil war") against [outlaws and Bourbonist guerrillas](/wiki/Post-unification_Italian_brigandage "Post-unification Italian brigandage"), such as the famous [Michelina Di Cesare](/wiki/Michelina_Di_Cesare "Michelina Di Cesare"), and against other Italian states' armies during the continuing wars of [unification](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification"). After the [monarchy ended](/wiki/1946_Italian_institutional_referendum "1946 Italian institutional referendum") in 1946, the army changed its name to become the modern [Italian Army](/wiki/Italian_Army "Italian Army") (*Esercito Italiano*).

Military unit

The **Royal Italian Army** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Regio Esercito*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Royal Army', **RE**) was the [land force](/wiki/Land_force "Land force") of the [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy"), established with the [proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Proclamation_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy "Proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy"). During the 19th century Italy started to unify into one country, and in 1861 [Manfredo Fanti](/wiki/Manfredo_Fanti "Manfredo Fanti") signed a decree creating the [Army of the Two Sicilies](/wiki/Army_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Army of the Two Sicilies"). This newly created army's first task was to defend its territorial gains against [Legitimists](/wiki/Legitimist "Legitimist") in southern Italy, who remained loyal to [Francis II of the Two Sicilies](/wiki/Francis_II_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Francis II of the Two Sicilies"). The Army of the Two Sicilies also waged what many modern historians now consider a [civil war](/wiki/Civil_war "Civil war") against [outlaws and Bourbonist guerrillas](/wiki/Post-unification_Italian_brigandage "Post-unification Italian brigandage"), such as the famous [Michelina Di Cesare](/wiki/Michelina_Di_Cesare "Michelina Di Cesare"), and against other Italian states' armies during the continuing wars of [unification](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification"). After the [monarchy ended](/wiki/1946_Italian_institutional_referendum "1946 Italian institutional referendum") in 1946, the army changed its name to become the modern [Italian Army](/wiki/Italian_Army "Italian Army") (*Esercito Italiano*).

Within the Royal Italian Army were the elite mountain military corporals, the [Alpini](/wiki/Alpini "Alpini"). The Alpini, which remain in existence today, are the oldest active mountain infantry in the world. Their original mission was to protect and secure Italy's northern mountain border that aligns with [France](/wiki/France "France") and [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"). This group emerged in World War I when a three-year campaign was fought against the [Austro-Hungarian](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary") [Kaiserjäger](/wiki/Kaiserj%C3%A4ger "Kaiserjäger") and the [German](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire") [Alpenkorps](/wiki/Alpenkorps_(German_Empire) "Alpenkorps (German Empire)"). In [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the Alpini fought alongside [Axis forces](/wiki/Axis_powers "Axis powers") in the [Eastern Front](/wiki/Eastern_Front_(World_War_II) "Eastern Front (World War II)") as well as the [Balkans campaign](/wiki/Balkans_campaign_(World_War_II) "Balkans campaign (World War II)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Origins")]

The *Regio Esercito* dates from the [proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Proclamation_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy "Proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy"), following the [unification of Italy](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification") in 1861 after most of the [Papal States](/wiki/Papal_States "Papal States") were seized. On 4 May 1861, [Manfredo Fanti](/wiki/Manfredo_Fanti "Manfredo Fanti") signed the creation decree, by which the new army was to replace the previous [Royal Sardinian Army](/wiki/Royal_Sardinian_Army "Royal Sardinian Army") and the [Army of the Two Sicilies](/wiki/Army_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Army of the Two Sicilies").

The first two tasks of the new organization were the repression of [brigandage](/wiki/Brigand "Brigand") in [southern Italy](/wiki/Southern_Italy "Southern Italy") against irregular and hit and run forces (mixed with bands of various criminals), who refused to accept the suppression of the [Kingdom of Two Sicilies](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Two_Sicilies "Kingdom of Two Sicilies"), and the [Third War of Italian Independence](/wiki/Third_War_of_Italian_Independence "Third War of Italian Independence"). On 20 September 1870, the IV Corps captured [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), which had remained under Papal control up until then.

On 8 February 1885, a corps of fewer than 1,000 soldiers landed at [Massaua](/wiki/Massaua "Massaua"), [Eritrea](/wiki/Eritrea "Eritrea"), starting the creation of an [Italian colonial empire](/wiki/Italian_Empire "Italian Empire"). The Italian advance of the [First Italo-Ethiopian War](/wiki/First_Italo-Ethiopian_War "First Italo-Ethiopian War") was halted at the [Battle of Adwa](/wiki/Battle_of_Adwa "Battle of Adwa") by overwhelming Ethiopian forces. The following year, as part of the Italian collaboration with the international pacification program after the revolt against the Turkish domination in [Cyprus](/wiki/Ottoman_Cyprus "Ottoman Cyprus"), another corps disembarked at [Candia](/wiki/Heraklion "Heraklion"). On 14 July 1900, another expeditionary force was constituted to suppress the [Boxer Rebellion](/wiki/Boxer_Rebellion "Boxer Rebellion") in [China](/wiki/China "China") in defense of the European protectorates.

On 3 October 1911, Italy invaded Libya as part of the [Italo-Turkish War](/wiki/Italo-Turkish_War "Italo-Turkish War"). The war against the [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") ended with the signing of the [First Treaty of Lausanne](/wiki/Italo-Turkish_War#Treaty_of_Ouchy "Italo-Turkish War") in [Ouchy](/wiki/Ouchy "Ouchy"), near [Lausanne, Switzerland](/wiki/Lausanne,_Switzerland "Lausanne, Switzerland").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### Military Justice in the Royal Italian Army

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Military Justice in the Royal Italian Army")]

In the Italian Royal Army, military justice was applied on the based on the 1870 Military Penal Code regulations. This regulation structure was very similar to the 1859 version which was inspired by the [Royal Sardinian Army](/wiki/Royal_Sardinian_Army "Royal Sardinian Army") penal code of 1840 which preceded the [Albertine Statute](/wiki/Albertine_Statute "Albertine Statute") of 1848. These regulations and factors were determined to be inadequate in the face of direct war violence. In total, 4,028 death sentences were passed in the Royal Italian Army whereas 2,967 were issued absentia where 750 were followed through and completed and 311 were not.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### World War I

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: World War I")]

Main articles: [Military history of Italy during World War I](/wiki/Military_history_of_Italy_during_World_War_I "Military history of Italy during World War I") and [Italian Front (World War I)](/wiki/Italian_Front_(World_War_I) "Italian Front (World War I)")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=). *(May 2019)* |

The Royal Italian Army's first experience with [modern warfare](/wiki/Modern_warfare "Modern warfare") was in [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"), from 1915–1918. The war was fought mostly on the [Italian Front](/wiki/Italian_Front_(World_War_I) "Italian Front (World War I)") in [Northern Italy](/wiki/Northern_Italy "Northern Italy"), costing the Italian Army serious casualties, including 246,133 killed, 946,640 wounded, 569,210 captured and 70,656 missing.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The Italian 35th Division served on the [Macedonian Front](/wiki/Macedonian_front "Macedonian front") as part of the [Allied Army of the Orient](/wiki/Allied_Army_of_the_Orient "Allied Army of the Orient").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Some Italian divisions were also sent to the [Western Front](/wiki/Western_Front_(World_War_I) "Western Front (World War I)"). In 1918, Italian troops saw intense combat during the German spring offensive.

Italian soldiers also saw action in campaigns against the Ottoman Empire in Africa and the Middle East such as in the [Senussi campaign](/wiki/Senussi_campaign "Senussi campaign").

Overall, the Royal Italian Army suffered 460,000[[9]](#cite_note-9) to 709,000[[10]](#cite_note-Mortara,_G_1925_P_28-10)[[11]](#cite_note-ITA,_14-18-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) killed and 947,000 to 1,050,000 wounded during World War I.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

### Interwar period

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Interwar period")]

Main article: [Italian Empire](/wiki/Italian_Empire "Italian Empire")

During the [Interwar period](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period"), the army was initially focused on [border security](/wiki/Border_security "Border security") in the [Alps](/wiki/Alps "Alps") and on the [Italian-Yugoslav border](/wiki/Italy%E2%80%93Slovenia_border "Italy–Slovenia border"). It supported [Benito Mussolini](/wiki/Benito_Mussolini "Benito Mussolini")'s [Fascist regime](/wiki/Fascist_Italy_(1922%E2%80%931943) "Fascist Italy (1922–1943)") because of its expansionist ideology and reversal of previous governments' defense cuts.[[14]](#cite_note-14) In the 1930s, the army participated in the [final subjugation of Libya](/wiki/Second_Italo-Senussi_War "Second Italo-Senussi War"), participated in the [invasion of Ethiopia](/wiki/Second_Italo-Abyssinian_War "Second Italo-Abyssinian War"), provided troops and materials for the Corps of Volunteer Troops (*[Corpo Truppe Volontarie](/wiki/Corpo_Truppe_Volontarie "Corpo Truppe Volontarie")*) to fight in the [Spanish Civil War](/wiki/Spanish_Civil_War "Spanish Civil War"), and participated in the [Italian invasion of Albania](/wiki/Italian_invasion_of_Albania "Italian invasion of Albania").

### World War II

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: World War II")]

Main articles: [Royal Italian Army during World War II](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army_during_World_War_II "Royal Italian Army during World War II") and [Military history of Italy during World War II](/wiki/Military_history_of_Italy_during_World_War_II "Military history of Italy during World War II")

The *[Regio Esercito](/wiki/Regio_Esercito_(World_War_II) "Regio Esercito (World War II)")* (Royal Army) was one of the [largest ground forces](/wiki/Military_history_of_Italy_during_World_War_II "Military history of Italy during World War II") in World War II, during which it was one of the pioneers of the use of [paratroopers](/wiki/Paracadutisti "Paracadutisti").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Many Italian divisions were reinforced by a [MVSN](/wiki/Blackshirts "Blackshirts") *Gruppo di Assalto* of two battalions due to the small size of the divisions.

In 1943, Italy surrendered and split into the [Italian Social Republic](/wiki/Italian_Social_Republic "Italian Social Republic"), which fielded its own army, the *Esercito Nazionale Repubblicano* ([National Republican Army](/wiki/National_Republican_Army_(Italian_Socialist_Republic) "National Republican Army (Italian Socialist Republic)")). On the other side was the *Esercito Cobelligerante del Sud* ([Italian Co-Belligerent Army](/wiki/Italian_Co-Belligerent_Army "Italian Co-Belligerent Army")), the army of the Italian Royalist forces, fighting on the side of the [Allies](/wiki/Allies_of_World_War_II "Allies of World War II") in southern Italy after the [Allied armistice with Italy in September 1943](/wiki/Armistice_of_Cassibile "Armistice of Cassibile").

The Kingdom was ultimately replaced by the [Italian Republic](/wiki/Italy "Italy") in the [1946 Italian institutional referendum](/wiki/1946_Italian_institutional_referendum "1946 Italian institutional referendum"), and the Royal Army accordingly changed its name to become the *Esercito Italiano* ([Italian Army](/wiki/Italian_Army "Italian Army")).

### Timeline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Italian_Army&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Timeline")]

* **1861** – The *Regio Esercito* dates from the [proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Proclamation_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy "Proclamation of the Kingdom of Italy"), following the unification of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") in 1861 after the [Papal States](/wiki/Papal_States "Papal States") were seized. On 4 May 1861, [Manfredo Fanti](/wiki/Manfredo_Fanti "Manfredo Fanti") signed the creation decree, by which the new army was to replace the previous [Royal Sardinian Army](/wiki/Royal_Sardinian_Army "Royal Sardinian Army") and the Army of the Two Sicilies. The first two tasks of the new organization were the repression of brigandage in southern Italy against irregular and hit and run forces (mixed with bands of various criminals), who refused to accept the suppression of the [Kingdom of Two Sicilies](/wiki/Kingdom_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Kingdom of the Two Sicilies"), and the [Third War of Italian Independence](/wiki/Third_Italian_War_of_Independence "Third Italian War of Independence").
* **1870** – On 20 September the IV Corps capture [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), which had remained under [Papal](/wiki/Pope "Pope") control up until then.
* **1885** – On 8 February fewer than 1,000 soldiers landed at [Massaua](/wiki/Massawa "Massawa"), Eritrea, starting the creation of an Italian colonial empire. The Italian advance was halted at the [Battle of Adwa](/wiki/Battle_of_Adwa "Battle of Adwa") by overwhelming [Ethiopian](/wiki/Ethiopia "Ethiopia") forces. The following year, as part of the [Italian](/wiki/Italy "Italy") collaboration with the international pacification program after the revolt against the [Turkish](/wiki/Turkic_peoples "Turkic peoples") domination in [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus "Cyprus"), another corps disembarked at Candia.
* **1900** – On 14 July another expeditionary force was constituted to suppress the Boxer Rebellion in China in defense of the European protectorates.
* **1911** – On 3 October Italy invaded Libya as part of the Italo-Turkish War. The war against the [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") ended with the signing of the First Treaty of [Lausanne](/wiki/Lausanne "Lausanne") in [Ouchy](/wiki/Ouchy "Ouchy"), near Lausanne, Switzerland.
* **1915–1918** – The Royal Italian Army's first experience with modern warfare was in [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"). The war was fought mostly on the [Italian Front](/wiki/Italian_Front_(World_War_I) "Italian Front (World War I)") in Northern Italy, costing the Italian Army serious casualties, including about 600,000 dead.
* **1918–1939** – During the [Interwar](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period") period, the army participated in the final subjugation of [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya"), participated in the invasion of [Ethiopia](/wiki/Ethiopia "Ethiopia"), provided troops and materials for the Corps of Volunteer Troops (*Corpo Truppe Volontarie*) to fight in the Spanish Civil War, and participated in the Italian invasion of Albania.
* **1940** – The Italian Royal Army had 1,630,000 men divided into 73 separate divisions. Out of these 73, there are 59 infantry divisions, six Alpini divisions, three Celere divisions, three armored divisions plus numerous Frontier Guard and coastal sector. The main cause of the Italian army's suffering was due to inadequate equipment, weaponry and leadership. This deficiency ultimately led to numerous defeats in the year 1940.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* The *Regio Esercito* (Royal Army) was one of the largest ground forces in World War II, during which it was one of the pioneers of the use of paratroopers.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Many Italian divisions were reinforced by a MVSN *Gruppo di Assalto* of two battalions due to the small size of the divisions.
* **1943** – Italy surrendered and split into the Italian Social Republic, which fielded its own army, the *[Esercito Nazionale Repubblicano](/wiki/National_Republican_Army "National Republican Army")* (National Republican Army). On the other side was the *Esercito Cobelligerante del Sud* (Italian Co-Belligerent Army), the army of the Italian Royalist forces, fighting on the side of the Allies in southern Italy after the Allied armistice with Italy in September 1943.
* **1946** – The Kingdom was ultimately replaced by the [Italian Republic](/wiki/Italian_Republic "Italian Republic") in 1946, and the Royal Army accordingly changed its name to become the *Esercito Italiano* (Italian Army).
